h2_adjacency.rationale: GIS mapping for power distribution is a spatial/data role with minimal direct connection to H2 value chain operations

transition_demand.rationale: Some demand for mapping H2 pipeline routes and RE plant siting, but GIS is a support function not directly scaled by H2

skill_transferability.rationale: GIS skills are general-purpose; would need significant domain retraining to work on H2 infrastructure planning

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: GIS mapping is increasingly automated with AI-based spatial analysis, satellite imagery processing, and auto-digitization

formalization_rate.rationale: GIS roles in Indian power distribution companies (DISCOMs) are typically formal positions within state utilities or contracted IT firms

scarcity_risk.rationale: India has abundant GIS/geospatial talent from geography and IT programs; not a bottleneck for H2

h2_adjacency.rationale: Meter reading and billing is a downstream consumer-facing utility role with no connection to H2 production or distribution

transition_demand.rationale: H2 scaling does not meaningfully increase demand for electricity meter readers; smart meters are reducing this role

skill_transferability.rationale: Meter reading and cash collection skills have almost no transferability to H2-specific technical work

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Smart meters, digital billing, and UPI/online payments are rapidly automating this role in India already

formalization_rate.rationale: Mix of formal DISCOM employees and outsourced contractual workers; many meter readers are on temporary contracts

scarcity_risk.rationale: Abundant low-skill labor pool; this role is actually shrinking due to smart meter rollouts

h2_adjacency.rationale: Cable jointing is electrical infrastructure work; H2 plants need power connections but this is general electrical not H2-specific

transition_demand.rationale: Green H2 plants need grid connections and dedicated RE power lines; cable jointers will see moderate demand from new energy infrastructure

skill_transferability.rationale: Electrical skills are transferable to H2 plant electrical systems but would need training on H2-specific safety and high-current electrolyser connections

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Cable jointing is highly manual fieldwork requiring dexterity and site judgment; very difficult to automate

formalization_rate.rationale: Many cable jointers work for DISCOMs or large contractors formally, but a significant portion are informal subcontracted workers

scarcity_risk.rationale: Skilled cable jointers exist in reasonable numbers for current grid work; demand may tighten moderately with H2 buildout

h2_adjacency.rationale: Chemical radiation technicians work in nuclear/power plants; some chemical handling overlap but radiation focus is distant from H2

transition_demand.rationale: Green H2 production does not involve radiation; this role's demand is driven by nuclear power, not H2

skill_transferability.rationale: Chemical handling, safety protocols, and process monitoring skills partially transfer to H2 chemical process environments

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Mix of automated monitoring systems and manual inspection/sampling that still requires human judgment

formalization_rate.rationale: Radiation technicians work almost exclusively in formal settings — nuclear plants, BARC, NPCIL, or licensed facilities

scarcity_risk.rationale: Niche specialization with limited training pipeline in India; moderate scarcity but not directly relevant to H2

h2_adjacency.rationale: Consumer energy metering is end-user focused; no direct role in H2 production, storage, or distribution

transition_demand.rationale: H2 scale-up may slightly increase industrial metering needs but consumer meter technicians are not significantly affected

skill_transferability.rationale: Basic electrical and instrumentation knowledge provides a foundation, but substantial retraining needed for H2 systems

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Smart meters with remote monitoring and IoT are rapidly replacing manual meter installation and calibration work

formalization_rate.rationale: Employed by DISCOMs and metering companies, mostly formal but with a significant outsourced/contract worker segment

scarcity_risk.rationale: Large existing workforce with ITI-level training; no scarcity concern for H2 purposes

h2_adjacency.rationale: Senior leadership in electricity sector; strategic decisions may touch H2 but the role itself is not in H2 operations

transition_demand.rationale: Directors overseeing electricity will increasingly need to understand H2 integration, green power procurement for electrolysis

skill_transferability.rationale: Energy sector leadership experience transfers well to H2 project oversight; domain knowledge gap is manageable at executive level

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Executive decision-making, stakeholder management, and policy navigation are very hard to automate

formalization_rate.rationale: Director-level positions are entirely formal — government utilities, CPSUs, or large private power companies

scarcity_risk.rationale: Senior energy executives are relatively scarce but not H2-specific; existing power sector leadership can pivot

h2_adjacency.rationale: HV electrical technicians work on power systems directly relevant to electrolyser power supply, transformer connections, and switchgear

transition_demand.rationale: H2 plants need HV connections for large electrolysers; demand will grow moderately as new plants commission

skill_transferability.rationale: HV electrical skills transfer well to H2 plant electrical infrastructure; needs H2 safety training and electrolyser-specific knowledge

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: HV work is hands-on, safety-critical fieldwork requiring physical presence and expert judgment; low automation potential

formalization_rate.rationale: HV technicians typically work for utilities, large EPC contractors, or power companies in formal roles with safety certifications

scarcity_risk.rationale: Moderate scarcity — India trains many electrical technicians but HV-certified specialists are fewer; demand growing across RE and H2

h2_adjacency.rationale: Energy auditors assess energy efficiency; relevant to H2 plant energy optimization but not directly in H2 operations

transition_demand.rationale: H2 certification schemes (ISCC, CertifHy) require energy accounting; auditors will be needed for green H2 compliance verification

skill_transferability.rationale: Energy measurement, efficiency analysis, and regulatory compliance skills transfer directly to H2 energy accounting and certification

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Data analysis portions are automatable but site audits, stakeholder interviews, and compliance judgment still need humans

formalization_rate.rationale: BEE-certified energy auditors in India are licensed professionals working in formal consulting or corporate roles

scarcity_risk.rationale: India has ~15,000 certified energy auditors; growing H2 certification demand will strain this pool, especially for H2-specific auditing

h2_adjacency.rationale: Power distribution engineers design and manage grid infrastructure; relevant to connecting H2 plants to RE supply but not core H2

transition_demand.rationale: Green H2 needs dedicated RE power corridors and grid connections; distribution engineers will see incremental demand

skill_transferability.rationale: Electrical engineering fundamentals transfer well; needs additional training on electrolyser load profiles and H2 plant power systems

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Design work partially aided by software but field engineering, fault analysis, and network planning need human expertise

formalization_rate.rationale: Engineers in Indian DISCOMs and power companies are formal employees, typically B.E./B.Tech qualified with structured roles

scarcity_risk.rationale: India produces many electrical engineers; power distribution is a well-supplied field, though experienced engineers are more scarce

h2_adjacency.rationale: General management in electricity is administrative/strategic; indirect connection to H2 through energy sector leadership

transition_demand.rationale: Some GMs will oversee H2-integrated energy projects, but this is a general management role not specifically driven by H2 demand

skill_transferability.rationale: Management and energy sector experience transfer to H2 project management; technical H2 knowledge gap is bridgeable

digital_automation_exposure.rationale: Senior management roles involving people management, strategy, and stakeholder relations are highly resistant to automation

formalization_rate.rationale: GM-level positions in Indian power sector are entirely formal — state utilities, NTPC, Adani Power, Tata Power, etc.

scarcity_risk.rationale: Experienced energy sector GMs are somewhat limited in number but not a specific H2 bottleneck; management talent is fungible
